title: Mayor Owens Announces City After Hours for Select Constituent Services
summary: Mayor Owens announced City After Hours, an extended hours pilot program launching July 6, 2026, extending Monday operations to 7 p.m. for four constituent service centers: City Payment Center, Municipal Violations Bureau, Department of Assessment, and Central Permit Office. The initiative aims to improve accessibility for residents unable to visit during standard business hours and will be evaluated for potential expansion.

political_signal.description: Mayor leadership prioritizes constituent service accessibility and flexibility; positions extended hours as responsive governance testing approach with planned evaluation for expansion.

processing_notes: Author listed as Unknown; press release issued from Office of Communications
Department of Assessment confirmed as entity; 'Department of Permits and Development' inferred to encompass Central Permit Office
Four service locations identified with specific addresses and parking information provided
24/7 online services mentioned as complementary offering; CIO should note digital service parity strategy
Pilot program structure suggests data collection/success metrics likely being tracked for future policy decisions
